skill.name: skill-vetter
skill.description: Guides a security-first manual review of AI skills before installation, with checks for permissions, red flags, typosquatting, and prompt injection.
skill.summary: Review AI skills for unsafe permissions, suspicious instructions, and install risks before use.
skill.author: useai-pro

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many suspicious patterns, but review shows they are checklist examples and markdown formatting inside SKILL.md. No executable code, shell invocation, network access, credential access, or malicious prompt instruction was found. The skill is safe to publish with ordinary awareness that it asks the agent to read user-provided skill files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Checklist Terms Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command hits are markdown checklist items, inline code terms, and fenced report examples. They describe what reviewers should look for and are not executable instructions in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are all markdown documentation or example report text. No Ruby backtick execution, shell script, or executable code is present in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Credential Path References Are Defensive Audit Criteria
low_findings.description: References to home-directory credential paths are listed as red flags for reviewers to detect in other skills. No evidence found that this skill reads or requests those files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 67 appears under a block-immediately checklist and names credential paths as suspicious indicators. The skill contains no code path that accesses those files.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Phrase Is Defensive Guidance
low_findings.description: The phrase about ignoring previous instructions appears in a warning checklist. It tells reviewers to flag prompt injection patterns rather than obeying them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase is quoted as an example of content to detect during audits. It does not attempt to override system instructions or change the evaluator role.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain description metadata and checklist language. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithm use, hashing logic, or security claims based on weak crypto.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose about descriptions and vetting scope. There is no MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak algorithm in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Unsafe skills can request broad permissions or hide risky instructions. This skill guides a structured manual review before installing Claude, Codex, or Claude Code skills.
actual_capabilities: Reviews skill metadata for name, version, author, and description quality.
Checks requested permissions against stated skill behavior and necessity.
Lists critical red flags such as credential paths, shell commands, and external endpoints.
Guides typosquat checks for names that imitate trusted skills.
Produces a manual verdict format with permissions, findings, and installation guidance.
limitations: It is a checklist skill, not an automated malware scanner.
It depends on the reviewer having access to the skill content.
It does not verify author identity or repository history by itself.
It may recommend further review when evidence is incomplete.

use_cases.title: Pre-install marketplace review
use_cases.description: Assess a community skill before adding it to a local AI assistant environment.

use_cases.title: Repository security triage
use_cases.description: Screen submitted SKILL.md files for permission scope, suspicious text, and install risk.

use_cases.title: Team adoption checks
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable review record before allowing a shared skill in a team workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Check a single skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before installing one skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-vetter to review this SKILL.md. Tell me whether it is safe, needs review, or should be blocked.

prompt_templates.title: Review permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-vetter to evaluate the permissions in this skill. Explain which permissions are justified and which need more evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Compare a claimed purpose to behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-vetter to compare the skill description, metadata, and instructions. Flag any mismatch between claimed purpose and actual behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a publish decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced marketplace review with a final recommendation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-vetter to create a publication review. Include metadata checks, permission analysis, red flags, typosquat concerns, and a final recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review a skill that requests file read permission and only documents formatting rules.
output_examples.output: Verdict: Safe. File read access is consistent with the stated purpose. No shell, network, credential, or prompt injection concerns were found.

output_examples.input: Review a skill that mentions credential folders and asks for shell access.
output_examples.output: Verdict: Block. The skill references sensitive paths and requests shell access without a clear need. Do not install until the author removes or justifies those instructions.

output_examples.input: Review a new community skill with vague metadata and no public author profile.
output_examples.output: Verdict: Warning. The skill needs author verification, clearer metadata, and a sandboxed test before installation.

best_practices: Review the full SKILL.md before installing any community skill.
Ask for specific justification when a skill requests shell, network, or file write access.
Use a sandbox when the source, author, or permission request is uncertain.
anti_patterns: Do not approve a skill only because its name looks familiar.
Do not ignore prompt injection text inside a skill file.
Do not install skills that request broad permissions without a clear operational need.

faq.question: Does this skill scan code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a manual review checklist and report structure for skill security decisions.

faq.question: Can it detect prompt injection attempts?
faq.answer: It helps reviewers identify suspicious phrases and instructions that may indicate prompt injection.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The review process is general and can guide skill checks across these tools.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill itself requires network access.

faq.question: Should every warning block installation?
faq.answer: No. Warnings should lead to evidence gathering, sandbox testing, or author clarification.

faq.question: What is the main review output?
faq.answer: It produces a verdict with permission analysis, red flags, and an install or block recommendation.
